message:
fix(ffs-core): coalesce adjacent btrfs prealloc rewrite segments (bd-qk5kv)

After the rewrite-segment refactor in commit 875623d, the
push_rewrite_segment path correctly preserved prealloc-vs-regular
extent shape, but it still emitted one Prealloc entry per call site.
For a fallocate that walks across multiple chunks of the same
prealloc extent (e.g. punch-hole leaves an inner prealloc range plus
a trailing prealloc range that is logically contiguous), this
produced two adjacent BtrfsExtentRewriteSegment::Prealloc records
that the downstream extent allocator then committed as two separate
prealloc records — fragmenting what should be a single allocation
and surfacing as the "btrfs zero-range allocation preservation"
failure tracked by bd-qk5kv.

Fix: extract a btrfs_push_prealloc_rewrite_segment helper that:

  - Drops zero-length segments outright (the original code already
    discarded zero-length writes elsewhere; this just makes the
    invariant local).
  - Looks at the last segment in the buffer; if it is a Prealloc
    record whose logical_offset + length exactly equals the new
    logical_offset, extends the previous record's length in place
    instead of pushing a new one. checked_add on both the previous
    end and the merged length so an overflow surfaces as
    InvalidGeometry rather than a silent wrap.
  - Falls through to the original push if the previous segment is
    not a directly-adjacent Prealloc.

Single-file change, no public API touched. Coverage from the two
unit tests added in 875623d is sufficient (their span happened not
to be adjacent, so they kept passing; this commit fixes the case
they did not exercise). bd-qk5kv stays in_progress for the matching
regression test that proves the merge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
